calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, FEES
Off Calendar May-07-2009 Text Ruling

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-10-2009 Continued To May-14-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with Debra Dolch (Former Trustee). Brian McCauley for and with Barbara Bond (Successor Trustee).

Mr. McCauley stated that discovery needed to be conducted and that discussions needed to take place. Ms. Rasch stated that all of the previous accountings had been approved by the Court, that Ms. Bond already had copies of all of the documents and that Ms. Bond waited one year to raise her objections. The Parties raised issues regarding payment of the bond premium. If the Court finds that there is a duplication of work, fees will be awarded and paid by Ms. Bond. The Court set the matter for an evidentiary hearing which is to be heard on May 14, 2009 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-24-2009 Continued To Mar-10-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch (Former Trustee). Brian McCauley for Barbara Bond (Successor Trsutee).

Mr. McCauley stated that he filed his objections this morning and that he had not received any of the records. Ms. Rasch argued that the accounting had been provided to previous Counsel more than a year ago, that all of the records had been picked up from Ms. Dolch, that no claims had been raised and asked the Court to disregard the objections as they were untimely. The Court continued the matter to March 10, 2009 for a hearing on the objections. Ms. Rasch will file a response.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Shannon.

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Robert Mosher's motion to compel production of records pursuant to Welfare and Institutions Code 15630 et seq. is Denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Trust Contest On Grounds Of Revocation And Petition To Transfer Trust Estate To Special Admin. Of Decedent'S Estate Purusant To P.C.S. 850
Off Calendar Mar-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay (Counsel for Robert Mosher, Objector); Nancy Rasch (Counsel for Debra Dolch, Special Administrator and Successor Trustee); David Baer (Counsel for Georgia Albert, Objector); Debra Dolch (Special Administrator and Successor Trustee - present in court).

After brief discussion and consideration as to whether the Trust contest was at issue, the Court referred both matters (the Will Contest, Case No. 289003, and the Trust Contest, Case No. 289070) to Dept. 206 for trial assignment, signing Minute Orders for both cases in open court.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

Demurrer To Trust Contest
Off Calendar Feb-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Randall Schmidt appearing for moving party Robert Mosher. David Baer appearing for Georgia Lee Albert. Mr. Schmidt responded to the tentative ruling and Mr. Baer replied. Court finds Bookasta is more on point than Kotyck. Tentative ruling stands. Demurrer is overruled.
Mr. Baer requested to have the Trust contest consolidated with the Will contest and not stayed. Mr. Schmidt has no problem with consolidation. Court will issue an order to consolidate both contest for trial. Matter is continued to 3/12 for status. Status on Will contest in case nunmber 289003 on 2/26 is also continued to 3/12. Mr. Baer will prepare order on demurrer and send it to Mr. Schmidt for approval as to form. Mr. Schmidt will have 10 days from date of order to answer.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Ron Sinclair CSR# 1170. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: J. Peralta.
